conversations.say: Maren, we need to set a clear disclosure boundary before contributors get treated as suspects by people hunting R.
conversations.offer: Firsthand insight into Night Desk workflows and routing slips
conversations.request: Agreement on a safe disclosure boundary to protect vulnerable contributors

conversations.say: Jules, claiming succession to R means nothing if contributors are exposed as collateral damage.
conversations.offer: Consideration of support if concrete protections are established
conversations.request: A clear statement of what obligations you owe to anonymous contributors

conversations.say: Arden, Veridian archives have resources, but any collaboration requires absolute source protection guarantees first.
conversations.offer: Bounded workflow evidence from our collective
conversations.request: Formal institutional protections and resources for Night Desk contributors

public_statement: Fifteen years of investigative work belongs to the labor of many hands, not a single myth. We will protect our contributors.

commitments.promise: Establish a joint disclosure boundary regarding collective labor before speaking to outsiders

correction: Clarified that Night Desk workflow represents collaborative labor rather than a single authorial mantle.
goal_progress: Initiated discussions on disclosure boundaries with Maren
Engaged Arden on archive access versus source protection
reasoning_summary: Tessa begins Section 1 focused on securing the Night Desk collective against fallout from R's anniversary. She reaches out to Maren to set a safe disclosure boundary, challenges Jules on succession obligations, and approaches Arden to trade bounded workflow evidence for concrete source protections.
